headline: University Autonomy Index Places Malaysia at 34 of 50 Countries
context: The European University Association's 2025 global autonomy index ranked Malaysia low on institutional self-governance.

cards.big: Malaysia ranked 34th out of 50 countries in university autonomy index 2025
cards.sub: Organizational staffing financial and academic autonomy all scored below median

cards.big: Vice-Chancellors of all 20 public universities are appointed by the Minister of Education
cards.sub: No public university has an independent governing board selection process

cards.big: Public universities cannot set tuition fees or create new programs without MOE approval
cards.sub: Approval process averages 18 months delaying response to industry needs

cards.big: QS World Rankings show only 5 Malaysian universities in top 200 in 2026
cards.sub: Autonomy constraints are linked to slower institutional improvement rates

cards.big: Universities controlled by politics produce research that serves politics not knowledge
Should public universities have independent boards free from ministerial appointment
